evidence: `src/features/gallery/hooks/useAdjacentPagePreloader.ts` combines debounce timing, query prefetch orchestration, preloading queue lifecycle, and error reporting in one hook, creating a broad ownership boundary.

`src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/hooks/useMediaGalleryItemState.ts` pulls in logging/toast infrastructure directly instead of using the shared `handleError` facade, while `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` exists as the intended central entrypoint.

Open-state orchestration is spread across many components via callback props (`onOpenChange`, `onInfoOpenChange`, `onShotSelectorOpenChange`, `setEcosystemTipOpen`), with repeated coordination logic in `CreativePartnerPane`, `InfoTooltip`, `ShotActions`, and modal/dialog components.

findings.summary: Adjacent page preloader owns too many cross-layer responsibilities
evidence: `useAdjacentPagePreloader` controls debounce, calculates pagination bounds, clears a global queue, invokes cross-page prefetch coordination, and wires error logging callback behavior in one hook.
The hook depends directly on shared singleton-style preloading service lifecycle (`clearQueue`, config access), coupling page-navigation timing to global preloading internals.
findings.suggestion: Split into a thin React hook plus a feature service. Keep the hook focused on subscribing to React state changes, and move queue lifecycle + prefetch policy into a dedicated gallery preloading orchestrator module with a narrower API.

findings.summary: Feature hook bypasses shared error boundary contract and couples to internals
evidence: `useMediaGalleryItemState` imports `normalizeAndLogError` and `notifyError` directly, then re-implements toast gating logic (`if (appError.showToast) notifyError(...)`).
A shared facade (`handleError`) already encapsulates normalize/log/toast/onError behavior, so this hook is coupled to low-level error-handling internals and duplicates policy.
findings.suggestion: Replace direct `normalizeAndLogError` + `notifyError` usage with `handleError` in this hook and similar feature hooks, so error policy remains centralized and ownership is predictable.

findings.summary: Dialog/tooltip open-state control is fragmented across feature and shared layers
evidence: Multiple components expose raw open-state callbacks with similar but separate semantics (`onOpenChange`, `onInfoOpenChange`, `onShotSelectorOpenChange`, and explicit `setEcosystemTipOpen/setEcosystemTipDisabled`).
`CreativePartnerPane` contains a custom controller hook that coordinates mobile/desktop tooltip policy while still requiring parent-owned setter props, indicating split ownership of one UI concern.
findings.suggestion: Define a small shared open-state controller pattern for overlays (e.g., `useOverlayState` + typed event contract) and apply it across dialogs/tooltips to keep ownership local and behavior consistent without ad-hoc setter prop chains.
